name: Shopify Dev MCP Server (Official)
what_it_does: Official Shopify Dev MCP server enabling AI agents to query Shopify's developer documentation, API references, CLI commands, and developer resources — providing context for agents building Shopify apps, themes, and integrations.
use_cases: Querying Shopify Admin API documentation for app development agents
Getting Storefront API schema and examples for storefront agents
Accessing Shopify CLI command references for developer tooling agents
Searching Shopify app development guides for code generation agents
Understanding webhook events and payloads for integration agents
Getting Shopify Liquid template documentation for theme development agents
not_for: Production Shopify store management (use shopify-mcp for that)
Actual API calls to Shopify stores
best_when: An AI coding agent needs documentation context about Shopify APIs and development — especially for building Shopify apps or themes.
avoid_when: You need to actually manage a Shopify store — this is a documentation MCP, not a management MCP.

auth.notes: Documentation access — no authentication required for public developer docs.

pricing.notes: Free developer documentation MCP. Shopify Partners account may be needed for some API features.

known_agent_gotchas: Documentation-only — cannot perform actual Shopify store operations
Shopify API versions change quarterly — docs may reference multiple versions
Admin API (OAuth) vs Public API (Storefront) have different auth and capabilities
GraphQL Admin API has query cost limits — agents should be aware
Shopify Plus features not available in lower plans
Theme development (Liquid) is Shopify-specific — different from standard template systems
agent_readiness.security_notes: No credentials required — public documentation. HTTPS enforced. Shopify's enterprise security infrastructure.

security_score_components.security_notes: No credentials needed. Public documentation. HTTPS enforced. Shopify infrastructure security.
